content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660              322-5901              322-6441              322-6444

April 28, 1981


Scott H. Howard
Assistant City Attorney
City Hall, Rm. 220
613 East Broadway Ave.
Glendale, CA 91205

Dear Mr. Howard:

     Thank you for your recent request for written advice relative
for the need for the members of the Housing Advisory Commission to
file conflict of interest statements (Statements of Economic In-
terests). Ordinance No. 4484, which established the Commission,
provides that:

     The Housing Authority shall provide for procedures for review
and recommendation, and may from time to time delegate any of
its functions to the Housing Commission.

     This provision in the ordinance, if activated, would permit the
Housing Advisory Commission to make decisions which could result in
a conflict of interest. This is true, even though, as you state,
to date none of their recommendations have been followed.

     The City's Conflict of Interest Code currently designates
these members and requires that they file Statements of Economic
Interests. Consequently, they must file so long as the City has a
Code which requires that they do so. However, inasmuch as the
Commission has not been delegated this authority and, in fact, its
recommendations are not routinely followed, then the City could
elect to not designate the Commission members. However, if at any
time in the future this authority is delegated or the Commission
begins to regularly make decisions which are regularly approved,
then a Code and filings would be required. See 2 Cal. Adm. Code
section 18700(a)(1).

     We hope that this letter is helpful to you in advising the
Commission members. Should you have further questions, please
contact us at (916) 322-5901.

                    Sincerely,

                    Robert E. Leideigh
                    Counsel
                    Legal Division

REL:kp

City of GLENDALE

OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY

CALIFORNIA

613 E. Broadway Ave., Rm. 220
Glendale, CA 91205
Telephone: (213) 956-2080

March 31, 1981

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Attn: Barbara Milman, Esq.

Subject: Request for Written Advice
re Conflict of Interests

Ms. Milman:

This office hereby requests your written advice regarding the
applicability of the Political Reform Act to our Housing Advisory
Commission.

On December 26, 1979, the City of Glendale created a Housing
Advisory Commission pursuant to Section 34291 of the California
Health and Safety Code. The function of the Commission was and is
to review and make recommendations on all matters that come before
the Housing Authority prior to the Housing Authority's action. A
copy of Ordinance No. 4484 creating said commission is attached
hereto for your review.

The members of said commission are non-salaried, volunteer
members whose advice is at times requested and given to the Housing
Authority.

The Housing Advisory Commission has, on at least three
occasions, given advice to the Housing Authority relative to the
need for housing in the City of Glendale. On all such occasions,
the Housing Authority has disregarded the advice of the Housing
Commission.

The City Attorney's office has opined that the Housing
Advisory Commission must file conflict of interest statements
pursuant to the Political Reform Act. Numerous commissioners have
expressed their concern for such an opinion and have indicated
their willingness to resign rather than file a conflict of interest
statement in a capacity which is unsalaried and, they believe,
purely advisory.

Your advice and assistance in this matter would be greatly
appreciated. If you desire any additional information regarding

the Housing Advisory Commission or the Housing Authority and their
respective duties and obligations, please do not hesitate to contact
the undersigned at your convenience.

Very truly yours,

FRANK R. MANZANO, CITY ATTORNEY

By [signature]
Scott H. Howard,
Assistant City Attorney

SHH:dd

Enclosure

AN ORDINANCE OF THE COUNCIL OF THE

HOUSING ADVISORY COMMISSION

4
WHEREAS, the Council heretofore has declared that there
5

WHEREAS, the Council has heretofore declared itself to

be the commissioners of the Housing Authority; and

body must be created to assist the Housing Authority; Now,

11
Therefore,

12
BE IT ORDAINED BY THE COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF GLENDALE:

13
SECTION 1. The Housing Advisory Commission of the City

14
of Glendale is hereby created under the authority of Section 34291

15
of the California Health and Safety Code.

16
SECTION 2. The function of said commission shall be

17
as set forth in Section 34292 of the California Health and Safety

18
Code, to wit: To review and make recommendations on all matters

19
to come before the Housing Authority prior to Authority's action,

20
except in matters which the Authority, by resolution, shall exclude

from its review. The Housing Authority shall provide for proce-

22
dures for review and recommendation, and may from time to time

23
delegate any of its functions to the Housing Commission.

24
SECTION 3. The Housing Advisory Commission shall be

25
comprised of eleven (11) commissioners who shall be appointed by a

26
majority vote of the Council. Each member of the Housing Advisory

27
Commission shall be a qualified elector of the City at the time of

28
appointment and during incumbency. Commissioners appointed shall

1 be from the following category and in the number of each category

2 as hereinafter indicated: One banker or savings and loan execu-

3 tive engaged in residential financing; one developer or real estate

4 person engaged in developing residential properties; one tenant

5 recipient of housing assistance; one homeowner at large; one renter

6 at large; one architect or engineer; one attorney with experience

7 in the real estate residential development field; and four quali-

8 fied electors of the City of Glendale at large. In addition,

9 there shall be one non-voting City staff person designated by the

10 City Manager.

11         SECTION 4. The persons appointed to the Housing Advisory

12 Commission shall be appointed for the following terms: Three per-

13 sons initially appointed shall be designated to serve for terms of

14 one year from the date of their appointment; three persons ini-

15 tially appointed shall be designated to serve two years from date

16 date of their appointment; three persons initially appointed shall

17 be designated to serve for three years from date of their appoint-

18 ment; and two person initially appointed shall be designated to

19 serve for four years from date of appointment. Successors of

20 commissioners shall be appointed for a term of four years and all

21 vacancies shall be filled for the unexpired term.

22         The Council, by a majority vote, shall appoint one of

23 the persons appointed as hereinabove provided to serve as interim

24 chairperson of the Housing Advisory Commission.

25         SECTION 5. The Housing Advisory Commission shall promul-

26 gate rules and regulations for the place, time and manner conducting

27 its regular meetings and submit same for approval by the Council.

28         SECTION 6. This ordinance shall take effect and be in

force on the thirtieth (30th) day after its passage.

Passed by the Council of the City of Glendale on the

23th day of __________ December __________, 1979.

sections.question: This office hereby requests your written advice regarding the applicablility of the Political Reform Act to our Housing Advisory Commission. On December 26, 1979, the City of Glendale created a Housing Advisory Commission pursuant to Section 34291 of the California Health and safety Code. The function of the Commission was and is to review and make recommendations on all matters that come before Housing Authority prior to the Housing Authority's action. The members of said commission are non-salaried, volunteer members whose advice is at times requested and given to the Housing Authority. Must members of the Housing Advisory Commission file conflict of interest statements (Statements of Economic Interests) pursuant to the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion: The City's Conflict of Interest Code currently designates these members and requires that they file Statements of Economic Interests. Consequently, they must file so long as the City has a Code which requires that they do so. However, inasmuch as the Commission has not been delegated this authority and, in fact, its recommendations are not routinely followed by the City, the City could elect to not designate Commission members. However, if at any time in the future this authority is delegated or the Commission begins to make decisions which are regularly approved, then a Code and filing requirements would be appropriate.
sections.question_synthetic: Must non-salaried volunteer members of the City of Glendale's Housing Advisory Commission file Statements of Economic Interests under the Political Reform Act, given that the Commission only reviews and makes recommendations to the Housing Authority, whose decisions the Commission has no power to make?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Housing Advisory Commission members must currently file Statements of Economic Interests because they are designated in the City's existing Conflict of Interest Code, even though their recommendations are not routinely adopted. However, the City could remove this requirement by not designating these members as long as they remain purely advisory with no delegated decision-making authority; if the Commission's role changes to regular decision-making authority in the future, designation and filing would then be required.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request from Glendale City Attorney (dated March 31, 1981) and the FPPC response letter (dated April 28, 1981), plus Ordinance No. 4484 creating the Housing Advisory Commission. Some text is degraded/OCR damaged. FPPC response advice is clearly articulated despite formatting issues. Extracted Q/C from the incoming request and FPPC response portions respectively.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: This office hereby requests your written advice regarding the applicablility of the Political Reform Act to our Housing Advisory Commission. On December 26, 1979, the City of Glendale created a Housing Advisory Commission pursuant to Section 34291 of the California Health and safety Code. The function of the Commission was and is to review and make recommendations on all matters that come before Housing Authority prior to the Housing Authority's action. The members of said commission are non-salaried, volunteer members whose advice is at times requested and given to the Housing Authority. Must members of the Housing Advisory Commission file conflict of interest statements (Statements of Economic Interests) pursuant to the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: The City's Conflict of Interest Code currently designates these members and requires that they file Statements of Economic Interests. Consequently, they must file so long as the City has a Code which requires that they do so. However, inasmuch as the Commission has not been delegated this authority and, in fact, its recommendations are not routinely followed by the City, the City could elect to not designate Commission members. However, if at any time in the future this authority is delegated or the Commission begins to make decisions which are regularly approved, then a Code and filing requirements would be appropriate.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to a request from the City of Glendale regarding whether members of a non-salaried Housing Advisory Commission must file conflict of interest statements. The FPPC advises that they currently must file because they are designated in the City's Conflict of Interest Code, but the City may revise its Code to not require filing if the Commission remains purely advisory without delegated authority.
